executive_summary: Scenario 'Rapid file encryption simulation' is classified as likely ransomware impact activity with critical severity at confidence 0.970. File activity reached 5 total event(s), including 3 rename event(s). ATT&CK coverage includes T1105, T1486.
key_evidence: File activity reached 5 total event(s), including 3 rename event(s).
Detected 3 file extension change event(s), consistent with ransomware impact behavior.
Observed 1 network connection event(s) during the scenario.
Triggered 3 detection rule(s), highest rule severity: critical.
Mapped to ATT&CK technique(s): T1105, T1486.

triggered_rules.title: Bulk Extension Rename
triggered_rules.description: Detects multiple extension-changing renames, a common impact signal during ransomware encryption.

triggered_rules.title: Mass File Changes in Short Window
triggered_rules.description: Detects bursty file modifications and renames consistent with encryption-for-impact activity.

triggered_rules.title: Suspicious Outbound Connection During File Churn
triggered_rules.description: Detects network egress paired with active file churn, which can indicate payload staging or coordination during ransomware execution.

mitre_attack_mapping.rationale: Mapped directly from triggered rule tag attack.t1105.
Mapped directly from triggered rule tag attack.t1486.

recommended_actions: Preserve volatile evidence and collect forensic artifacts.
Assess impacted user files and block further write activity if possible.
Inspect outbound connections for potential command-and-control or staging activity.
analyst_notes: Objective: Simulate fast encryption-like file churn across multiple directories with a suspicious outbound connection.
Most relevant ATT&CK techniques: T1105, T1486.
